planned_changes: Add CSV exports of the primary calculation sheets (one CSV per sheet used to compute thermal, budget, and composite scores) and attach them as additional artifacts.
Include an OOXML text extract (or formulas.txt) that exposes the formulas used in key cells, so automated tools can inspect the logic without relying on openpyxl.

Update expert_memo.md with a new 'Reproducibility & Cell Mapping' section that lists exact sheet names and cell references for all headline numbers (e.g., 'SheetCalc!B8 = R2 device_loss_W', 'Scoring!C12 = O3 composite score formula'), plus a brief reproduction procedure.
Optionally include screenshots of the formula bar for the most critical cells if any viewer cannot parse OOXML; also include a plain-text summary of the same formulas.
Add an explicit provenance statement in the memo confirming that only the embedded packet data were used and listing any additional files included in the revision.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve traceability and verifiability of spreadsheet calculations to address the grader/verifier limitation that prevented programmatic inspection of .xlsx formulas.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Strong, conservative expert memo with clear methods, assumptions, sensitivity checks, and a well-justified recommendation (O3). All required deliverables are present and the memo documents 'needs_review' items appropriately. The only verification gap is that the .xlsx workbook formulas and in-spreadsheet cell-level citations could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable), leaving formula-level traceability unverified.
